title: Change the CSV delimiter
author: vedran-maestral
body: Currently when I import the CSV file, CSV expects values to be delimited with a , (comma). How can I change it to work with ; (semi column)?

comments.author: kgajowy
comments.body:
```
The CSV parser uses fast-csv to read the CSV file. The options passed into the read functions above is also passed to fast-csv for parsing of the csv data. Please refer to the fast-csv README.md for details.
```
As `fast-csv` states:
```
delimiter=',': If your data uses an alternate delimiter such as ; or \t.
NOTE When specifying an alternate delimiter you may only pass in a single character delimiter
```

comments.author: frankiDotNet
comments.body: Any chance to do this with csv.writeBuffer?

comments.author: frankiDotNet
comments.body:
Sorry didn't find it because the declared export methods are missing.

comments.body:
Any update ?
This is an annoying piece. In browser we have to use writeBuffer and need to change csv options. Giving options to writeBuffer doesnt work right now.

comments.author: eml-transdev
comments.body:
I managed to bypass the issue by changing the delimiter in the ParserOptions class from node modules installed package.  
In this file the delimiter is hardcoded to comma, therefore the delimiter specified in the option object will not be taken into accoun.
